message:
refactor: adopt io::Error::other and .inspect_err for clippy idioms

Two one-liners following the clippy 1.87 idiom guidance:

- src/cli/commands/history.rs: replace
    io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::Other, "injected")
  with
    io::Error::other("injected")
  (clippy::io_other_error). Behaviour identical — io::Error::other
  wraps the same ErrorKind::Other with the payload.

- src/cli/commands/sync.rs: write_manifest_atomically was using
    .map_err(|e| { let _ = fs::remove_file(&temp_path); e })?
  to run a cleanup side effect while preserving the original error.
  Swapped to .inspect_err(|_| { let _ = fs::remove_file(&temp_path); })?
  (clippy::manual_inspect). Avoids the explicit rebind of `e` and
  is clearer that the map_err wasn't transforming the error.

Unit tests unchanged; both paths still exercise the rename-then-
cleanup flow.
